title: Endless Runner
description: A 2D side-scrolling endless runner arcade game with gravity-based jumping, AABB collision detection, procedural obstacle spawning, progressive speed ramps, scrolling grid parallax, and localStorage high-score persistence.
author: Girish Madarkar
features: Gravity kinematics: per-frame vy += 0.6 when !grounded, ground clamp at GROUND_Y - h snaps player, jump applies vy = -10.5 impulse
One-touch input: mousedown, touchstart (with preventDefault), Space, and ArrowUp all dispatch doJump() guarded by grounded flag — multi-jump lock
AABB collision: a.x < b.x+b.w && a.x+a.w > b.x && a.y < b.y+b.h && a.y+a.h > b.y tested every frame between player and each obstacle
Procedural obstacle queue: 60% spawn chance per interval, two types (ground-level and elevated hanging), culled at x + w < -20
Speed ramp: BASE_SPEED 4 + floor(frameCount / 600) × 0.6, spawn interval 60 - speed×2 (min 28 frames) for progressive difficulty
Scrolling parallax grid: vertical/horizontal lines at 40px spacing with x-offset = -(frameCount × speed × 0.1) % gridSize for motion illusion
Running leg animation: grounded state drives leg offset via Math.sin(legPhase) × 3 for oscillating stride effect
Crash screen shake: CSS @keyframes crashShake 0.35s triggered via class toggle + reflow hack, game-over modal shows distance + final speed
localStorage erBest key: written on crash if distance exceeds stored record, displayed on boot and during gameplay
